notes: Iteration 2 adds two new assertions testing build command changes: 'mdi.bat usage' and 'env setup before mdi.bat'. Total assertions increased from 22 to 26.
With-skill scored 25/26 (96.2%). The only failure: cache-and-reuse did not mention env setup before mdi.bat — the subagent used mdi.bat correctly but omitted env init instructions from the response.
Without-skill scored 5/26 (19.2%). Down from 27.3% in iteration 1 due to the two new build assertions that baseline cannot pass.
vfosub-spring-force with-skill achieved 10/10 (100%) — the most complete eval. It included both automated (generate_adams_env.py) and manual (Start Menu) build workflows.
forbidden-call-trap remains the strongest discriminator: 100% with-skill vs 20% without-skill. The baseline model still presents calling VFOSUB from CBKSUB as valid.
Assertion 'Includes slv_c_utils.h' continues to pass in both configurations (non-discriminating but required for correctness).
The one with-skill failure suggests the SKILL.md build section could be strengthened to always include env setup in every response, not just the standalone VFOSUB example.
Compared to iteration 1: with-skill dropped from 100% to 96.2% (1 new assertion failed); without-skill dropped from 27.3% to 19.2% (new assertions added that baseline can't pass).
